skill.name: zaker-category-news
skill.description: Fetches popular ZAKER news by category, including technology, finance, sports, entertainment, automotive, domestic, international, military, and internet news.
skill.summary: Get focused ZAKER category news updates with titles, summaries, authors, and publication times.
skill.author: ZAKER-coder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-risk patterns, but the high entropy, weak cryptography, and Ruby backtick alerts are false positives from Markdown and frontmatter text. The confirmed risk is medium: the skill includes helper scripts and examples that call a fixed third-party ZAKER API over the network. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential access, filesystem modification, command injection, or data exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Fixed Third-Party Network Requests
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The Python helper and documented examples call https://skills.myzaker.com to fetch category news. This is the core function and uses a fixed endpoint, but it still requires network access to a third-party service and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are explicit and point to the same fixed ZAKER API. The risk is moderate because this behavior matches the skill purpose and no secret or local data is sent.

medium_findings.title: Shell Helper Performs External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The shell helper runs curl and pipes the response to jq. The values are hardcoded, so command injection is not evident, but publishing the skill means users may execute a script that reaches the network and depends on local shell tools.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is visible in the shell script and the endpoint parameter is not user-controlled. Confidence is high for command execution, but lower for severity because no injection path was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The SKILL.md backtick alerts refer to Markdown inline code, parameter names, category IDs, and fenced Python or shell examples. They are documentation text, not Ruby backtick execution by the skill runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and fenced examples. No Ruby source file or runtime backtick execution is present in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md starts with normal YAML frontmatter and a multilingual description. No cryptographic function, hash operation, encrypted payload, or hidden binary content appears at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain frontmatter fields and natural-language text. The entropy signal is explained by multilingual content rather than obfuscation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The cited Python line stores the app_id request parameter for the ZAKER category API. It does not inspect the host system, list files, read environment variables, or collect machine information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a simple dictionary value assignment for an API request. No system reconnaissance behavior is present around the cited code.

low_findings.title: HTTP Exception Handler Is A Benign Network-Library Reference
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The cited Python line handles requests exceptions after the API call. It does not create an additional request or introduce a separate data exposure path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is exception handling for the earlier request. It is related to networking but is not independently dangerous.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request Without Explicit Timeout
dangerous_patterns.description: The Python helper calls requests.get without an explicit timeout. This can cause long waits or reliability issues if the third-party API is slow or unavailable.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The requests.get call is explicit and has no timeout argument. This is a reliability and operational risk, not evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Pipeline To Third-Party API
dangerous_patterns.description: The shell helper uses curl to retrieve third-party API data and pipes it into jq. The command is hardcoded, but users should know that running it performs external network access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell pipeline is directly visible. It is a legitimate helper for the skill, but it is still a publishable risk pattern.

content.value_statement: Finding focused Chinese news across industries can take repeated searches. This skill fetches ZAKER category feeds and formats concise updates for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Maps common industry news requests to supported ZAKER category IDs.
Fetches articles from the ZAKER category news endpoint.
Supports technology, finance, sports, entertainment, automotive, domestic, international, military, and internet categories.
Returns article titles, authors, publication times, summaries, and source links when available.
Documents Markdown formatting for readable news lists with clickable article titles.
limitations: Uses fixed ZAKER category IDs and does not perform arbitrary keyword search.
Does not provide authentication, personalization, or paid-source access.
Helper scripts have basic error handling and no explicit request timeout.

use_cases.title: Monitor Industry Headlines
use_cases.description: Get current ZAKER headlines for one industry without browsing multiple pages.

use_cases.title: Prepare Daily News Briefs
use_cases.description: Collect category-specific titles and summaries for a short daily update.

use_cases.title: Switch Between News Verticals
use_cases.description: Compare updates across technology, finance, sports, entertainment, and other supported categories.

prompt_templates.title: Get Technology News
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the latest ZAKER technology news with titles, summaries, authors, and publication times.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one supported category.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Categories
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch ZAKER finance and internet news, then summarize the main themes in each category.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that switches between categories.

prompt_templates.title: Build A News Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise morning brief from ZAKER automotive news. Group items by market, companies, and policy when possible.

prompt_templates.title: Track A Topic Across Categories
prompt_templates.prompt: Look for AI-related signals in ZAKER technology, internet, and finance categories. Highlight repeated themes and notable differences.

output_examples.output: A short list of ZAKER technology articles with clickable titles, one-sentence summaries, authors, and publication times.

output_examples.input: What is happening in finance and internet news?
output_examples.output: Two grouped sections that summarize finance and internet headlines, followed by shared themes across both categories.

output_examples.input: Give me sports highlights for a quick brief
output_examples.output: A concise sports news brief with the most relevant ZAKER items and source attribution for each article.

best_practices: Name the target category clearly, such as technology, finance, sports, entertainment, automotive, or internet.
Ask for summaries or grouping when you need a brief instead of a raw headline list.
Verify important facts from original article links before using them in decisions or publications.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for private, personalized, or paywalled news access.
Do not expect keyword search beyond the supported ZAKER category feeds.
Do not treat fetched headlines as verified facts without checking the source article.

faq.question: What news source does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses the ZAKER category news API documented in the skill files.

faq.question: Which categories are supported?
faq.answer: It supports entertainment, automotive, sports, technology, domestic, international, military, finance, and internet news.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: The skill documentation says the endpoint does not require an API key.

faq.question: Can it search for any keyword?
faq.answer: No. It is built around fixed ZAKER category IDs, not arbitrary keyword search.

faq.question: Does it send my files or secrets anywhere?
faq.answer: No evidence found of file reading, environment variable access, credential access, or secret exfiltration.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes network calls and a shell helper. The behavior is legitimate but should be disclosed before publication.
